summary: Phase 2 Attendance Features - All tests passed. Backend APIs (attendance marking, stats, mobile availability sync) working correctly. Frontend UI (InlineAttendance in training sessions, player profile attendance stats, mobile schedule with Going/Not Going buttons) verified and functional.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Future_Training session (2026-04-15) and TEST_Opponent fixture (2026-04-20) for U12 group to enable availability testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 2 attendance features fully tested. Key endpoints: POST/GET /api/admin/events/{event_id}/attendance, GET /api/admin/attendance/stats?player_id={id}, POST /api/mobile/availability (syncs to event_attendance), GET /api/mobile/my-availability. Mobile schedule page shows Going/Not Going buttons when clicking on a specific day with events. The 'Upcoming' section shows status badges but buttons are in day detail view.

backend: POST /api/admin/events/{event_id}/attendance - marks attendance with responses array
GET /api/admin/attendance/stats?player_id={id} - returns player attendance stats including training sessions
POST /api/mobile/availability - syncs to both availability and event_attendance collections with source='mobile'
GET /api/mobile/my-availability - returns all availability for linked players
Parent dashboard events include training sessions and fixtures with type field
frontend: Training session expanded detail shows InlineAttendance panel with player list
InlineAttendance shows going/not-going buttons per player with count summary (0 going, 1 not going, 3 no response)
Player profile shows 'ΠΑΡΟΥΣΙΕΣ' section with attendance rate, progress bar, and event count
Mobile SchedulePage shows calendar with green dots on event days
Mobile SchedulePage shows 'Πάω'/'Δεν πάω' buttons when clicking on a day with events
Going/Not Going buttons toggle correctly and show current status with visual feedback
Status badges update in Upcoming section after toggle

mocked_apis.Twilio SMS: OTP returned in otp_debug field of request-otp response (simulated mode)
